content.text:
<user_query>
lets fix the flow of the edit session. 

On clicking edit it should simply edit and on editing success, it can land back to the session list again.

Entire session list item clickable to view the session
</user_query>

Exploring the session list and edit flow in the frontend.

[REDACTED]

Implementing: redirect to the session list after a successful save on the edit page; adding a session detail route for row clicks; restructuring `SessionList` so the main row area links to that detail view while keeping the grip, Edit, and Delete separate.

[REDACTED]

input.new_string:
<Link href={`/programs/${programId}/sessions`} className={cn(buttonVariants({ variant: "outline" }))}>
        Back to list
      </Link>

content.text:
Here is what changed: ### 1. **Save on edit → back to list** After a successful `PATCH`, the edit page now navigates to the program’s session list instead of calling `router.refresh()` and staying on the form. ```150:153:frontend/src/app/(dashboard)/programs/[id]/sessions/[sessionId]/edit/page.tsx return; } router.push(`/programs/${programId}/sessions`); } ``` ### 2. **Row opens a session “view”** There was no non-edit URL for a session, so a read-only **session detail** route was added at `/programs/[id]/sessions/[sessionId]` (`frontend/src/app/(dashboard)/programs/[id]/sessions/[sessionId]/page.tsx`). It loads the same `GET /sessions/:id` data and shows instructor, duration, tags, and media, with **Edit** and **Back to Sessions**. ### 3. **List row: big click target for view** In `SessionList`, the **title + meta** block is a single `Link` to that detail page (`flex-1` so it uses the space between the drag handle and the actions). The **grip** stays its own control; **Edit** and **Delete** stay separate so you do not nest links and drag still only starts from the handle. ```61:108:frontend/src/components/sessions/SessionList.tsx const viewHref = `/programs/${programId}/sessions/${session.id}`; return (
  • ...
    Edit ...
    • ); ``` **New session** still sends you to the edit screen after create; only **Save** on edit returns to the list. If you want **create** to go straight to the list (or to the new detail page), say how you’d like that to behave. [REDACTED]

Making each program row match the session list: a flex-1 `Link` to sessions for the main content; Edit and Delete in a separate action column.

[REDACTED]
